subfeature.purpose: Let users narrow Jobs results through understandable filter controls and reversible filter-editing actions.
subfeature.userValue: Users can shape the job list without losing confidence about what is currently active, pending, or cleared.
userGoals: Open filtering controls from the Jobs search page.
Choose filter values that meaningfully narrow results.
Apply or discard filter edits intentionally.
Clear filters and return to broader browsing.
inScopeBehavior: Validation and recovery for filter input
outOfScopeBehavior: Results card rendering details unrelated to active filters.
Map marker behavior except where filter changes affect map results.
Job application workflow after selecting a job.
entryPoints: User clicks Filters from Jobs search.
User opens a saved URL with active filters.
User returns to Jobs with filters already active.
exitPoints: User applies filters and returns to results.
User discards edits and remains at prior results.
User clears filters and returns to broad browsing.
constraints: Filter UX must distinguish active browsing constraints from provisional edits.
Destructive or broad reset behavior must be explicit enough for users to understand.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
